_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of compliance assessment methodologies, including control testing, evidence gathering, gap analysis, and findings documentation standards.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-K-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-T-008 (RM, cosine: 0.552), OP-T-010 (OP, cosine: 0.529).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.1/10 (raw 0.309). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [develop, guidelines, implementation] vs WIDAI-only terms [analysis, assessment, compliance, control, documentation, evidence] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.876 (P=0.905, R=0.848). P exceeds R by 0.057 — the DCWF element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 16 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:21.3%, N:77.1%, C:1.6%). No dominant classification for "Develop guidelines for implementation." vs "Knowledge of compliance assessment methodologies, including control testing, evi" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.566 > STS 0.309 (gap +0.257). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [develop, guidelines, implementation]. WIDAI terms: [analysis, assessment, compliance, control, documentation, evidence].
